description: A clean, minimalist designed data-visualization card titled 'Federal Employment' in large bold black sans-serif at the top, on a plain white background. It is a single navy-blue line chart plotting total federal employment month-by-month across the x-axis (labeled 'January 2025' through 'November 2025', rotated 45 degrees) against a y-axis ranging from 2,750,000 up to 3,050,000 in 50,000 increments. The line starts near 3,015,000 in January 2025 and declines steadily, then drops sharply between September and October 2025 to about 2,750,000, flattening near 2,745,000 by November. The simple typography and lone descending line are designed to dramatize a steep cut in the federal workforce. Per the tweet, it claims federal employment is 'now at the lowest level since 2014 — down by 271,000 jobs since President Trump took office. Promises made, promises kept.' Messaging technique: a stark statistics chart used as proof of a kept campaign promise to shrink government. Target audience: small-government conservatives and anti-bureaucracy voters.
